summary: Added deterministic XCUITest smoke for the three Continue source kinds the source-coverage triage flagged as missing (trainingSession, carriedFocusItem, writingNote). The maintained smoke suite now covers source visibility for all six composer-backed Continue sources. No routing, action, screenshot, device, or TestFlight proof was added; no product behavior changed. Three new launch arguments, three new seed functions, and three new tests; the seeds use the production FileItemListRepository / FileTodayEntryRepository paths so the composer, admission, and ranking pipelines run exactly as in the live app.
recommended_next_reason: Source visibility is now complete (6/6 composer sources). The next queued slice in the UI proof roadmap classifies expected routes for each source before adding route tests; screenshot, device, and TestFlight lanes remain deliberately downstream.

contract_status_changes.before: The maintained XCUITest smoke covered source visibility for three of the six composer-backed Continue sources: currentFocus / .focusItem (plus a Done action), activeHomeProtocolRun / .homeProtocolRun (plus active-run routing), and activeHomeTask / .homeTask (plus routing into Home). The source-coverage triage flagged dueTodayTraining, carriedForwardFocus, and inProgressWriting as missing source-visibility proof. The Continue UI Source Coverage table in today.md listed those three rows with `Needed proof` populated.
contract_status_changes.after: The maintained XCUITest smoke now covers source visibility for all six composer-backed Continue sources. Three new launch arguments (--owlory-ui-seed-due-today-training-continue-item, --owlory-ui-seed-carried-forward-focus-continue-item, --owlory-ui-seed-in-progress-writing-continue-item) drive deterministic seed paths that exercise the production FileItemListRepository / FileTodayEntryRepository surfaces; PatternEngine.computeCarryForward is exercised live for the carried-forward Focus case via four consecutive seeded daily entries. Three new tests assert each row's deterministic accessibility identifier (today.continue.item.trainingSession.<UUID>, today.continue.item.carriedFocusItem.<UUID>, today.continue.item.writingNote.<UUID>) and the seeded title. The today.md UI source coverage table now reads `None for source visibility.` in the Needed proof column for all six sources, and docs/workflows/ui-testing-hygiene.md documents the new seed arguments.

residual_risks: The carried-forward Focus seed depends on PatternEngine.computeCarryForward producing a stalled streak >= 3 from four consecutive seeded entries. If a future refactor changes the streak definition (e.g. requires non-equal createdFromDate or strict carryForward array membership rather than focusThree presence), this test would silently start asserting against currentFocus instead of carriedFocusItem. The test's deterministic source-kind identifier guards against this drift loudly.

These are source-visibility tests only. They do not exercise routing, Done/Defer/Drop actions, screenshots, or device flows. Source visibility passing does not imply correct destination routing for the three new sources; the routing matrix triage owns that classification.

The trainingSession seed dates the session at today's startOfDay. If a future Today rule narrows due-today admission (e.g. requires a specific time window beyond planned status and same calendar day), the seed must follow suit. The narrow scope here is intentional; the slice does not introduce a TrainingSession admission contract.

The writingNote seed uses stage .capture; isInProgressWritingCandidate admits any stage that is not .published or .archived. If the contract later tightens (e.g. requires non-empty body), the seed already provides a body so it should still pass, but the assertion only proves source visibility, not stage-specific behavior.

The seed paths reset app-local Owlory and legacy Trajectory application-support directories before writing; if a Continue source is later persisted in a new directory not covered by resetAppSupport, the seed isolation could leak. Audit resetAppSupport against any new persistence root before claiming UI smoke for sources that touch it.
